titles.en: Chapter 65 – the Time for Night Prayer
Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih
Chapter 65 – the Time for Night Prayer

segments: : «كَانَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ إِذَا صَلَّى اَلْعِشَاءَ أَوَى إِلَى فِرَاشِهِ فَلَمْ يُصَلِّ شَيْئاً حَتَّى يَنْتَصِفَ اَللَّيْلُ.
arabic.text: : «كَانَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ إِذَا صَلَّى اَلْعِشَاءَ أَوَى إِلَى فِرَاشِهِ فَلَمْ يُصَلِّ شَيْئاً حَتَّى يَنْتَصِفَ اَللَّيْلُ.

segments:
Hadith.1375 - Ubayd ibn Zurarah narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
"The Messenger of Allah (sw), after performing the Isha prayer, would retire to his bed and would not perform any additional prayer until midnight."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.1375 - Ubayd ibn Zurarah narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
"The Messenger of Allah (sw), after performing the Isha prayer, would retire to his bed and would not perform any additional prayer until midnight."

narratorChain.raw: 1375 - رَوَى عُبَيْدُ بْنُ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ

parts.text: أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ

titles.en: Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih
Chapter 65 – the Time for Night Prayer

segments: 1376 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «وَقْتُ صَلاَةِ اَللَّيْلِ مَا بَيْنَ نِصْفِ اَللَّيْلِ إِلَى آخِرِهِ.
arabic.text: 1376 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «وَقْتُ صَلاَةِ اَللَّيْلِ مَا بَيْنَ نِصْفِ اَللَّيْلِ إِلَى آخِرِهِ.

segments:
Hadith.1376 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"The time for the night prayer (salat al-layl) is from midnight until its end."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.1376 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"The time for the night prayer (salat al-layl) is from midnight until its end."

titles.en: Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih
Chapter 65 – the Time for Night Prayer

segments: «لاَ اِقْضِ بِالنَّهَارِ فَإِنِّي أَكْرَهُ أَنْ يُتَّخَذَ ذَلِكَ خُلُقاً.
arabic.text: «لاَ اِقْضِ بِالنَّهَارِ فَإِنِّي أَكْرَهُ أَنْ يُتَّخَذَ ذَلِكَ خُلُقاً.

segments:
Hadith.1377 - Umar ibn Hanzalah said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
"I have spent eighteen nights intending to rise (for the night prayer) but did not get up. Should I then pray at the beginning of the night?"
He (as) replied: "No, make it up during the day, for I dislike it becoming a habitual practice."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.1377 - Umar ibn Hanzalah said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as):
"I have spent eighteen nights intending to rise (for the night prayer) but did not get up. Should I then pray at the beginning of the night?"
He (as) replied: "No, make it up during the day, for I dislike it becoming a habitual practice."

narratorChain.raw: 1377 - وَقَالَ عُمَرُ بْنُ حَنْظَلَةَ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: إِنِّي مَكَثْتُ ثَمَانِيَ عَشْرَةَ لَيْلَةً أَنْوِي اَلْقِيَامَ فَلاَ أَقُومُ أَ فَأُصَلِّي أَوَّلَ اَللَّيْلِ قَالَ

parts.text: 1377 - وَقَالَ عُمَرُ بْنُ حَنْظَلَةَ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: إِنِّي مَكَثْتُ ثَمَانِيَ عَشْرَةَ لَيْلَةً أَنْوِي اَلْقِيَامَ فَلاَ أَقُومُ أَ فَأُصَلِّي أَوَّلَ اَللَّيْلِ قَالَ

titles.en: Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih
Chapter 65 – the Time for Night Prayer

segments: : قُلْتُ لَهُ إِنَّ رَجُلاً مِنْ مَوَالِيكَ مِنْ صُلَحَائِهِمْ شَكَا إِلَيَّ مَا يَلْقَى مِنَ اَلنَّوْمِ وَقَالَ لِي إِنِّي أُرِيدُ اَلْقِيَامَ لِصَلاَةِ اَللَّيْلِ فَيَغْلِبُنِي اَلنَّوْمُ حَتَّى أُصْبِحَ فَرُبَّمَا قَضَيْتُ صَلاَتِي اَلشَّهْرَ اَلْمُتَتَابِعَ أَوِ اَلشَّهْرَيْنِ أَصْبِرُ عَلَى ثِقَلِهِ فَقَالَ «قُرَّةُ عَيْنٍ وَ اَللَّهِ قُرَّةُ عَيْنٍ وَ اَللَّهِ وَ لَمْ يُرَخِّصْ فِي اَلْوَتْرِ أَوَّلَ اَللَّيْلِ» فَقَالَ «اَلْقَضَاءُ بِالنَّهَارِ أَفْضَلُ.
arabic.text: : قُلْتُ لَهُ إِنَّ رَجُلاً مِنْ مَوَالِيكَ مِنْ صُلَحَائِهِمْ شَكَا إِلَيَّ مَا يَلْقَى مِنَ اَلنَّوْمِ وَقَالَ لِي إِنِّي أُرِيدُ اَلْقِيَامَ لِصَلاَةِ اَللَّيْلِ فَيَغْلِبُنِي اَلنَّوْمُ حَتَّى أُصْبِحَ فَرُبَّمَا قَضَيْتُ صَلاَتِي اَلشَّهْرَ اَلْمُتَتَابِعَ أَوِ اَلشَّهْرَيْنِ أَصْبِرُ عَلَى ثِقَلِهِ فَقَالَ «قُرَّةُ عَيْنٍ وَ اَللَّهِ قُرَّةُ عَيْنٍ وَ اَللَّهِ وَ لَمْ يُرَخِّصْ فِي اَلْوَتْرِ أَوَّلَ اَللَّيْلِ» فَقَالَ «اَلْقَضَاءُ بِالنَّهَارِ أَفْضَلُ.

segments:
Hadith.1378 - It is reported from Mu'awiyah ibn Wahb that He said:
"I said to him (Abu Abdullah (as)):
'One of your followers, who is among your righteous adherents, complained to me about the difficulty he experiences with sleep. He said to me: ‘I wish to rise for the night prayer (salat al-layl), but sleep overcomes me until I wake up in the morning. Sometimes I make up my prayer continuously for a month or two and endure the difficulty.’
Imam (as) said: 'By Allah (swt), it is a source of delight, by Allah (swt), it is a source of delight!' But he did not permit performing the Witr (single unit of the night prayer) at the beginning of the night.
Imam (as) said: 'Making it up during the day is better.'"
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.1378 - It is reported from Mu'awiyah ibn Wahb that He said:
"I said to him (Abu Abdullah (as)):
'One of your followers, who is among your righteous adherents, complained to me about the difficulty he experiences with sleep. He said to me: ‘I wish to rise for the night prayer (salat al-layl), but sleep overcomes me until I wake up in the morning. Sometimes I make up my prayer continuously for a month or two and endure the difficulty.’
Imam (as) said: 'By Allah (swt), it is a source of delight, by Allah (swt), it is a source of delight!' But he did not permit performing the Witr (single unit of the night prayer) at the beginning of the night.
Imam (as) said: 'Making it up during the day is better.'"

narratorChain.raw: وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ وَهْبٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ:

titles.en: Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih
Chapter 65 – the Time for Night Prayer

segments: : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلصَّلاَةِ فِي اَلصَّيْفِ فِي اَللَّيَالِي اَلْقِصَارِ صَلاَةِ اَللَّيْلِ فِي أَوَّلِ اَللَّيْلِ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ نِعْمَ مَا رَأَيْتَ وَ نِعْمَ مَا صَنَعْتَ.
arabic.text: : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلصَّلاَةِ فِي اَلصَّيْفِ فِي اَللَّيَالِي اَلْقِصَارِ صَلاَةِ اَللَّيْلِ فِي أَوَّلِ اَللَّيْلِ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ نِعْمَ مَا رَأَيْتَ وَ نِعْمَ مَا صَنَعْتَ.

segments:
Hadith.1379 - Abdullah ibn Muskan reported from Layth al-Muradi who said:
"I asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about praying the night prayer during the summer, when the nights are short, and performing it at the beginning of the night. Imam (as) said: 'Yes, what you have considered is good, and what you have done is good.'"
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.1379 - Abdullah ibn Muskan reported from Layth al-Muradi who said:
"I asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about praying the night prayer during the summer, when the nights are short, and performing it at the beginning of the night. Imam (as) said: 'Yes, what you have considered is good, and what you have done is good.'"

narratorChain.raw: 1379 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ لَيْثٍ اَلْمُرَادِيِّ قَالَ

titles.en: Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih
Chapter 65 – the Time for Night Prayer

segments: : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَخَافُ اَلْجَنَابَةَ فِي اَلسَّفَرِ أَوْ فِي اَلْبَرْدِ فَيُعَجِّلُ صَلاَةَ اَللَّيْلِ وَ اَلْوَتْرَ فِي أَوَّلِ اَللَّيْلِ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ.
arabic.text: : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَخَافُ اَلْجَنَابَةَ فِي اَلسَّفَرِ أَوْ فِي اَلْبَرْدِ فَيُعَجِّلُ صَلاَةَ اَللَّيْلِ وَ اَلْوَتْرَ فِي أَوَّلِ اَللَّيْلِ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ.

segments:
Hadith.1380 - He (Layth al-Muradi) said:
"I asked him (Abu Abdullah (as)) about a man who fears becoming ritually impure (janabah) while traveling or in cold weather and thus performs the night prayer (salat al-layl) and Witr at the beginning of the night.
Imam (as) said: 'Yes.'"
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.1380 - He (Layth al-Muradi) said:
"I asked him (Abu Abdullah (as)) about a man who fears becoming ritually impure (janabah) while traveling or in cold weather and thus performs the night prayer (salat al-layl) and Witr at the beginning of the night.
Imam (as) said: 'Yes.'"

titles.en: Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih
Chapter 65 – the Time for Night Prayer

segments: قَالَ: «صَلِّ صَلاَةَ اَللَّيْلِ فِي اَلسَّفَرِ مِنْ أَوَّلِ اَللَّيْلِ فِي اَلْمَحْمِلِ وَ اَلْوَتْرَ وَ رَكْعَتَيِ اَلْفَجْرِ.
arabic.text: قَالَ: «صَلِّ صَلاَةَ اَللَّيْلِ فِي اَلسَّفَرِ مِنْ أَوَّلِ اَللَّيْلِ فِي اَلْمَحْمِلِ وَ اَلْوَتْرَ وَ رَكْعَتَيِ اَلْفَجْرِ.

segments:
Hadith.1381 - Abu Jarir ibn Idris narrated from Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) who said:
"Perform the night prayer (salat al-layl) during travel at the beginning of the night, in the carriage, and also perform the Witr and the two units (rak'ahs) of the Fajr prayer."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.1381 - Abu Jarir ibn Idris narrated from Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) who said:
"Perform the night prayer (salat al-layl) during travel at the beginning of the night, in the carriage, and also perform the Witr and the two units (rak'ahs) of the Fajr prayer."

narratorChain.raw: 1381 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو جَرِيرِ بْنُ إِدْرِيسَ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ

parts.text: أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ

titles.en: Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih
Chapter 65 – the Time for Night Prayer

segments: : «لَيْسَ مِنْ عَبْدٍ إِلاَّ وَ هُوَ يُوقَظُ فِي لَيْلَتِهِ مَرَّةً أَوْ مَرَّتَيْنِ فَإِنْ قَامَ كَانَ ذَلِكَ وَ إِلاَّ جَاءَ اَلشَّيْطَانُ فَبَالَ فِي أُذُنِهِ أَ وَ لاَ يَرَى أَحَدُكُمْ أَنَّهُ إِذَا قَامَ وَ لَمْ يَكُنْ ذَلِكَ مِنْهُ قَامَ وَ هُوَ مُتَخَثِّرٌ ثَقِيلٌ كَسْلاَنُ.
arabic.text: : «لَيْسَ مِنْ عَبْدٍ إِلاَّ وَ هُوَ يُوقَظُ فِي لَيْلَتِهِ مَرَّةً أَوْ مَرَّتَيْنِ فَإِنْ قَامَ كَانَ ذَلِكَ وَ إِلاَّ جَاءَ اَلشَّيْطَانُ فَبَالَ فِي أُذُنِهِ أَ وَ لاَ يَرَى أَحَدُكُمْ أَنَّهُ إِذَا قَامَ وَ لَمْ يَكُنْ ذَلِكَ مِنْهُ قَامَ وَ هُوَ مُتَخَثِّرٌ ثَقِيلٌ كَسْلاَنُ.

segments:
Hadith.1382 - Al-'Ala' reported from Muhammad ibn Muslim from one of the Imams (as) who said:
"There is no servant except that they are awakened during the night once or twice. If they rise (for prayer), it will be for their benefit; otherwise, Shaytan comes and urinates in their ear. Do you not see that when one rises and has not risen for prayer, they do so sluggishly and heavily, feeling lazy?"
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.1382 - Al-'Ala' reported from Muhammad ibn Muslim from one of the Imams (as) who said:
"There is no servant except that they are awakened during the night once or twice. If they rise (for prayer), it will be for their benefit; otherwise, Shaytan comes and urinates in their ear. Do you not see that when one rises and has not risen for prayer, they do so sluggishly and heavily, feeling lazy?"

narratorChain.raw: 1382 - وَ رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ

titles.en: Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih
Chapter 65 – the Time for Night Prayer

segments: : «إِنِّي لَأَمْقُتُ اَلرَّجُلَ يَأْتِينِي فَيَسْأَلُنِي عَنْ عَمَلِ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَيَقُولُ أَزِيدُ كَأَنَّهُ يَرَى أَنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَصَّرَ فِي شَيْءٍ وَ إِنِّي لَأَمْقُتُ اَلرَّجُلَ قَدْ قَرَأَ اَلْقُرْآنَ ثُمَّ يَسْتَيْقِظُ مِنَ اَللَّيْلِ فَلاَ يَقُومُ حَتَّى إِذَا كَانَ عِنْدَ اَلصُّبْحِ قَامَ يُبَادِرُهُ بِصَلاَتِهِ.
arabic.text: : «إِنِّي لَأَمْقُتُ اَلرَّجُلَ يَأْتِينِي فَيَسْأَلُنِي عَنْ عَمَلِ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَيَقُولُ أَزِيدُ كَأَنَّهُ يَرَى أَنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَصَّرَ فِي شَيْءٍ وَ إِنِّي لَأَمْقُتُ اَلرَّجُلَ قَدْ قَرَأَ اَلْقُرْآنَ ثُمَّ يَسْتَيْقِظُ مِنَ اَللَّيْلِ فَلاَ يَقُومُ حَتَّى إِذَا كَانَ عِنْدَ اَلصُّبْحِ قَامَ يُبَادِرُهُ بِصَلاَتِهِ.

segments:
Hadith.1383 - Al-Hasan al-Sayqal reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said: "I despise a man who comes to me asking about the actions of the Messenger of Allah (sw) and then says, 'Should I add more?' as if he sees that the Messenger of Allah (sw) was lacking in something.
And I despise a man who has read the Qur'an and then wakes up during the night but does not rise (for prayer) until, when it is near dawn, he hastily performs his prayer."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.1383 - Al-Hasan al-Sayqal reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said: "I despise a man who comes to me asking about the actions of the Messenger of Allah (sw) and then says, 'Should I add more?' as if he sees that the Messenger of Allah (sw) was lacking in something.
And I despise a man who has read the Qur'an and then wakes up during the night but does not rise (for prayer) until, when it is near dawn, he hastily performs his prayer."

narratorChain.raw: 1383 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ اَلصَّيْقَلُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ

parts.text: أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ

titles.en: Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih
Chapter 65 – the Time for Night Prayer

segments: : «مَا نَوَى عَبْدٌ أَنْ يَقُومَ أَيَّةَ سَاعَةٍ نَوَى فَعَلِمَ اَللَّهُ تَبَارَكَ وتَعَالَى ذَلِكَ مِنْهُ إِلاَّ وَكَّلَ بِهِ مَلَكَيْنِ يُحَرِّكَانِهِ تِلْكَ اَلسَّاعَةَ.
arabic.text: : «مَا نَوَى عَبْدٌ أَنْ يَقُومَ أَيَّةَ سَاعَةٍ نَوَى فَعَلِمَ اَللَّهُ تَبَارَكَ وتَعَالَى ذَلِكَ مِنْهُ إِلاَّ وَكَّلَ بِهِ مَلَكَيْنِ يُحَرِّكَانِهِ تِلْكَ اَلسَّاعَةَ.

segments:
Hadith.1384 - Abu Hamzah al-Thumali reported from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
"There is no servant who intends to rise (for prayer) at any hour they intend, and Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, knows this intention from them, except that He assigns two angels to move them at that hour."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.1384 - Abu Hamzah al-Thumali reported from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
"There is no servant who intends to rise (for prayer) at any hour they intend, and Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, knows this intention from them, except that He assigns two angels to move them at that hour."

narratorChain.raw: 1384 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو حَمْزَةَ اَلثُّمَالِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ

titles.en: Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih
Chapter 65 – the Time for Night Prayer

segments: : «إِذَا غَلَبَ اَلرَّجُلَ اَلنَّوْمُ وَ هُوَ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ فَلْيَضَعْ رَأْسَهُ فَلْيَنَمْ فَإِنِّي أَتَخَوَّفُ عَلَيْهِ إِنْ أَرَادَ أَنْ يَقُولَ اَللَّهُمَّ أَدْخِلْنِي اَلْجَنَّةَ أَنْ يَقُولَ اَللَّهُمَّ أَدْخِلْنِي اَلنَّارَ.
arabic.text: : «إِذَا غَلَبَ اَلرَّجُلَ اَلنَّوْمُ وَ هُوَ فِي اَلصَّلاَةِ فَلْيَضَعْ رَأْسَهُ فَلْيَنَمْ فَإِنِّي أَتَخَوَّفُ عَلَيْهِ إِنْ أَرَادَ أَنْ يَقُولَ اَللَّهُمَّ أَدْخِلْنِي اَلْجَنَّةَ أَنْ يَقُولَ اَللَّهُمَّ أَدْخِلْنِي اَلنَّارَ.

segments:
Hadith.1385 - Ays ibn al-Qasim reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"If a man is overcome by sleep while he is in prayer, let him put his head down and sleep, for I fear that if he wishes to say, 'O’ Allah (swt), admit me to Paradise,' he might instead say, 'O’ Allah (swt), admit me to the Fire.'"
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.1385 - Ays ibn al-Qasim reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) who said:
"If a man is overcome by sleep while he is in prayer, let him put his head down and sleep, for I fear that if he wishes to say, 'O’ Allah (swt), admit me to Paradise,' he might instead say, 'O’ Allah (swt), admit me to the Fire.'"

narratorChain.raw: 1385 - وَ رَوَى عِيصُ بْنُ اَلْقَاسِمِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ

parts.text: أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ

titles.en: Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih
Chapter 65 – the Time for Night Prayer

segments:
اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «لاٰ تَقْرَبُوا اَلصَّلاٰةَ وَ أَنْتُمْ سُكٰارىٰ حَتّٰى تَعْلَمُوا مٰا تَقُولُونَ»
قَالَ «مِنْهُ سُكْرُ اَلنَّوْمِ.
arabic.text:
اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ «لاٰ تَقْرَبُوا اَلصَّلاٰةَ وَ أَنْتُمْ سُكٰارىٰ حَتّٰى تَعْلَمُوا مٰا تَقُولُونَ»
قَالَ «مِنْهُ سُكْرُ اَلنَّوْمِ.

segments:
Hadith.1386 - Zakariyya al-Naqqad reported from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding the words of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic:
"Do not approach prayer while you are intoxicated until you know what you are saying" (Surah An-Nisa 4:43).
Imam (as) said: "This includes the intoxication of sleep."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.1386 - Zakariyya al-Naqqad reported from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding the words of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic:
"Do not approach prayer while you are intoxicated until you know what you are saying" (Surah An-Nisa 4:43).
Imam (as) said: "This includes the intoxication of sleep."

narratorChain.raw: 1386 - وَ رَوَى زَكَرِيَّا اَلنَّقَّاضُ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: فِي قَوْلِ
